Reflections Productions — Rebrand Proposal

Prepared by Avant Garde Media · July 2026
01 · The Rebrand Game Plan

Fifty years on stage.
A brand that finally shows it.

Reflections Productions Inc is a ~50-year-old South Florida concert staging and event production company. Real fleet — seven APEX mobile stages plus a VIP deck. Real clients — Hialeah 4th of July, Big Orange NYE Miami. Real capability — JBL concert audio, lighting, LED video, MOJO crowd barriers, backline, logistics. And a brand and website that actively undersell all of it: a heritage company presenting far below its capability. The rebrand’s job is to make 50 years of production credibility visible.

The Problem, In One Image
“REFLECTIONS PRODUCITON INC”
“REFLECTIONS PRODUCITON INC”

Quoted verbatim from the live site: the company name is misspelled in the sitewide navigation, rendered in giant type on every page of the site, including the 404 page — 8 times in the homepage HTML alone. The portfolio lives at /potfolio/. It is exactly the kind of detail that quietly erodes trust with promoters and municipalities — and everything else in this dashboard flows from the same root cause: the brand has not had a dedicated owner.

1.1

Brand Strategy

Positioning / The Claim

South Florida’s production backbone — the company that’s been putting the region’s biggest moments on stage for five decades. Heritage + horsepower.

Voice / How It Sounds

Confident, technical, unfussy. Spec-sheet precision with showtime energy. No agency fluff — a promoter should read one page and know these people can hang a headliner’s rig.

The Reframe / Why Now

The core problem is not capability, it’s presentation. Broken images, empty carousels, a blog feed for a homepage, no quote funnel. Fix the frame and the substance sells itself.

1.2

Tagline Candidates

Evolved from your existing line “South Florida’s Creative Source” — keep the regional claim, sharpen the promise. To be refined together in the next round.

Candidate A · Recommended Test “The Stage Behind South Florida’s Biggest Nights” Positions them as the invisible backbone of every marquee event — heritage claim + promoter appeal.
Candidate B “Fifty Years On Stage” Shortest possible proof of trust. Works hard on trucks, merch, and social avatars.
Candidate C · Heritage Line “South Florida’s Creative Source” Their current line, retained as a secondary heritage signature rather than the lead claim.
1.3

The Identity Play — Homage, Not Erasure

Keep

  • The palm silhouette from the 1970s oval badge
  • The horizontal reflection lines — light on water
  • The mark sitting on the waterline

Modernize

  • Clean geometry, drop the oval badge clutter
  • Bold condensed all-caps wordmark
  • Correct spelling — everywhere, forever

The Twist

The reflection lines double as stage-light beams / EQ bars — one motif that says both “Reflections” and “concert production.” It becomes a system-wide device (dividers, photo treatments, loading states) — you’re looking at it working throughout this dashboard.

Bridges from your current teal so the rebrand feels like an upgrade, not an identity transplant.

1.5

Typography

The Stage Behind
South Florida’s Biggest Nights
ABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQRSTUVWXYZ 0123456789
Archivo Black
Display · headlines, stat numbers, wordmark class. Honors the condensed type they already use — executed properly.

Archivo carries body copy and specifications: 50′ × 40′ performance area, hydraulic lift, 55,165 lbs trailer weight, JBL line arrays, FOH & monitors. A clean grotesk that reads like a spec sheet and never fights the display face.

Archivo 600 handles labels, table heads and UI — the weight you are reading across this dashboard.

1.6

Site Architecture — Built & Planned

From blog feed to sales machine — and no longer a plan. 21 pages are built and running. Solid boxes are live in the current build; outlined boxes are the proposed next wave.

Home

The funnel mouth — everything drives to a quote
  • Video hero (their drone footage) + watch-with-sound
  • Trust strip · “What do you need?” pathways
  • Check-your-date availability form
  • Testimonials · live Instagram reel · rates teaser

Stage Fleet 10 PAGES

A product catalog, not a list
  • Fleet overview + 8-model comparison table
  • 8 stage pages — real specs, crowd-size guidance, engineering chips
  • 5040 Full Web Spec — the 70-page PDF, torn down & readable
  • Web specs for the other 7 stages — next wave
  • Interactive 3D stage viewer — next wave

Production

One partner, not six vendors
  • Six services w/ real gear lists (JBL, Absen, GrandMA2, MOJO)
  • Deep-linkable sections for quoting

Rates NEW

Nobody else in this industry publishes prices
  • 5 full-width sections + jump tiles
  • Crew labor · power & site · DJ systems · movie packages · stages
  • All rates recovered from their own archive

Our Work 5 PAGES

Proof, with receipts
  • Portfolio with six real productions
  • 4 clickable case studies — galleries + aftermovie embeds
  • Formula E case study — pending client material

About · Quote · More

The story + the close
  • About: 1975→2026 timeline, brands, hiring, values
  • Get a Quote: full form, prefilled by the date-checker
  • On-brand 404 · sitemap · social icons sitewide
  • FAQ · Media hub (12 videos) · market landing pages — next wave

The blog stays — the “Backstage” content is real SEO equity for terms like mobile stage rental Miami — but demoted to a News section, never the homepage.

02 · Site Audit

Every page crawled.
Every problem logged.

A full crawl of reflectionsproductions.com — every published page and post, every image, every link and spec sheet, checked and catalogued. This is the evidence layer under the whole rebrand.

85
Pages & posts crawled
84
Issues logged
42
Images captured
5
Dead spec PDFs on-domain
~20
Theme-demo pages still published
2.1

Top Findings

01

The company name is misspelled on every page High

The sitewide navigation reads “REFLECTIONS PRODUCITON INC” — 8× in the homepage HTML alone, rendered on every page including the 404. The portfolio URL is /potfolio/. The brand’s first impression is a spelling error.

02

About page appears to be an unedited AI draft High

The About copy appears to be AI-generated draft content published unintentionally: it carries 9 links to pages that don’t exist on the site, each tagged ?utm_source=chatgpt.com. A 50-year company story deserves a finished page, not a draft with dead links.

03

Working SEO document published as a public page Medium

A raw ChatGPT “SEO Analysis” (scorecards, keyword lists, 90-day plan) is live at /seo/, hotlinking its images from images.openai.com — working material — not visitor content — sitting in the public sitemap, on links that can expire.

04

All 5 on-domain spec PDFs are dead High

Every stage spec sheet hosted on reflectionsproductions.com returns HTML instead of a PDF. The 9 working spec sheets live on external domains apex.miami / mobilestages.miami — the product catalog depends on someone else’s hosting.

05

~20 theme-demo & placeholder pages live Medium

“Sample Page” (linking to /wp-admin/), lorem-ipsum template pages, five blog-layout demos, a Timeline, a Slider — plus a full WooCommerce shop/cart/checkout with no functioning store. All indexed, all public.

06

Heavy duplicate pages Medium

Three published pages titled “GALLERY”, and two each of My Account, Graphic Arts, Cross Rentals, Mobile Stage Conference 2023, Random Media and Blog — cannibalizing each other in search and confusing navigation.

07

WAF soft-404s hide real 404s from Google Medium

The firewall answers every unknown URL with an HTTP 200 “Verifying…” challenge page. Search engines can’t see real 404s, standard sitemap URLs are intercepted, and crawlers get blocked from real pages.

08

Brand & contact chaos Medium

Two brand names in rotation (Reflections Productions vs Mobile Stages Miami), two street addresses in Princeton FL, two contact emails, and two phone numbers formatted 5+ different ways — a citation-consistency nightmare for local SEO.

09

Title tags wasted Low

The flagship APEX 5040 post’s title tag is literally Concert Stage Rental 786-504-2369 — a phone number where the brand and product name should be. Post slugs like /5040-2/stages/ betray deleted earlier versions.

10

14 near-empty published pages Medium

Pages with 0–10 words of content are live and indexed — “THROUGH THE YEARS” (0 words), “STAGE DIMENSIONS AND PERMITS” (10 words), Music Player, YouTube Player, shop pages and more. Thin content dragging the whole domain down.

What we captured

The full text of every page and post (not just the metadata above), all 42 images, and the complete fleet specifications for every APEX stage and the Captain’s VIP Deck — stored and ready to pour into the new architecture. The rebuild starts from a complete copy of everything you own, not from scratch.

3.2

Every image recovered from the live site, thumbnailed. Full-resolution originals are stored alongside.

04 · Identity Concepts

Three directions.
One shared bloodline.

Each direction carries forward the DNA of the original 1970s badge: the palm silhouette, the horizontal reflection lines that read as light on water, and the sense of a mark sitting on the waterline. What’s gone is the oval badge clutter; what’s new is that the reflection bars now double as stage-light beams and EQ meters — water and concert production in one gesture.

01
Mirror Palm

The most literal homage: a single-weight palm on its island, standing on a solid waterline whose broken, fading bars below are simultaneously the tree’s reflection and a decaying EQ read-out. A coral coconut-sun marks the crown.

Mark · Stage Black
Light Variant · Warm Sand
02
Sundown Spotlight
Recommended

A sunset-coral disc that reads as both Florida sun and stage spotlight, with a minimal palm knocked out of the light and the lower half sliced into horizontal water bars — the whole show contained in one round beam. Our recommendation: the most ownable mark at small sizes — it holds as a 16px favicon and a social avatar where the other two thin out.

Mark · Stage Black
Light Variant · Warm Sand
03
Reflection R

An abstract monogram: the R built entirely from horizontal reflection bars — scanlines become letterform — its leg dissolving into teal water while a tiny coral frond sprouts from the top, the palm reduced to a single grace note.

Mark · Stage Black
Light Variant · Warm Sand
R2

Round 2 — Palm as R

Direction after Round 1: Sundown Spotlight was the favorite — with the note that “the tree almost looks like an R.” These three concepts make that real: the palm (the 1975 homage, the South Florida signature) and the company initial fused into one mark. And a second callback — the original 1975 badge carried two palms on an island; concept 06 carries them forward.

04
Sundown R
Evolves the Client Favorite

Sundown Spotlight, one step further: same coral disc, same water bars — but the palm is redrawn so the trunk becomes the R’s stem, the frond crown curls into its bowl, and one frond kicks out to the waterline as the leg. The R is discoverable, not shouted: you see it, then you can’t unsee it.

Mark · Stage Black
Light Variant · Warm Sand
05
Palm Monogram

Typography-first: a bold, Archivo-Black-weight R that sprouts palm fronds from the top of its bowl, carries a tiny coral sun as its counter, and dissolves into reflection bars below the baseline. The strongest avatar and favicon of the set.

Mark · Stage Black
Light Variant · Warm Sand
06
Badge R

The heritage option: a modern circular badge nodding to the original 1970s oval. Twin palms — as on the 1975 badge — rise from an island and fuse into the R, standing on a waterline that runs the badge’s full width, with REFLECTIONS PRODUCTIONS · EST. 1975 set around the ring.

Mark · Stage Black
Light Variant · Warm Sand

Type Pairing

Reflections Productions
ARCHIVO BLACK · DISPLAY   /   Archivo · body & specifications
Archivo Black + Archivo
Wordmark class · wide-tracked Archivo secondary line, as set in the lockups above.

Bold condensed all-caps display for headlines honors the condensed type they already use — executed properly. The clean grotesk carries body and specs with spec-sheet precision.

05 · Live Site + Roadmap

Here’s your
new front door.

The homepage concept that started it all, built from Reflections Productions’ real fleet, real events and real content — running live below — and the full 21-page build has since gone far beyond it (see Tab 07). Something a promoter screenshots and sends to their partner.

The new site — live preview (links open the deployed pages) reflectionsproductions.com · proposed
5.1

Engagement Roadmap

PhaseWorkstreamDeliverableStatus
1Audit & content captureFull site crawl — 85 pages/posts, 42 images, fleet specs, 84-issue log✓ Complete
2Content dashboardThis exhibit — every page, image and issue in one interactive view✓ Complete
3Identity6 logo concepts across two rounds — incl. the palm-as-R series (Tab 04)✓ Complete
4Brand systemPalette, typography, reflection-line device — codified and demonstrated✓ Complete
5Working siteFull 21-page site, 5 revisions deep — video hero, rates, case studies, web specs, mobile-hardened — deployed to a live preview URL✓ Complete
6Phase 2 — Launch & beyondProduction launch (domain, forms, redirects, analytics) + next wave: remaining web specs, market pages, resources hub, content engine, 3D viewerProposal attached
5.2

Launch Checklist & The Next Wave

The rebuild itself is done — 21 pages, five revisions deep. What remains splits into launch-day items (flipping the new site live) and the next wave (Tab 07 has the full picture).

Point the domain & go liveLaunch day

reflectionsproductions.com moves to the new build. The PRODUCITON typo, the AI-drafted pages, the ~20 theme-demo pages and /potfolio/ all disappear in the same hour — with 301 redirects preserving every old URL’s equity.

Wire the formsLaunch day

Quote form and date-checker connected to a live inbox with same-day response — plus analytics and Search Console from day one.

Consolidate brands & contacts

One name, one address, one email, one phone format — on-site, on Google Business Profile, and across citations. (Two street addresses are currently published.)

Real quotes & missing photos

Swap the sample testimonials for collected client quotes, confirm the Formula E story, and shoot the gaps — per-model stage photos, crew portraits, client logos.

The next wave

Web-spec teardowns for the remaining 7 stages · market landing pages (municipalities / festivals / broadcast) · resources hub · FAQ & media hub · the 90-day content engine (Tab 06) · interactive 3D stage viewer.

06 · Content Strategy

Ninety days of
owning the stage.

A 13-week editorial engine for July 15 – October 15, 2026 — built on the real fleet, the real clients and the 50-year story. Every week feeds the same funnel: search → proof → Quick Quote. It ends where the rebrand begins: the anniversary moment in week 13.

Primary
1

Inbound quote requests

Quote-form submissions and tracked calls. Every post, guide and GBP update points at Quick Quote.

2

South Florida SEO authority

Own the “mobile stage rental Miami / South Florida / Florida” keyword family with fleet pages and organizer guides.

3

The 50th-anniversary moment

A weekly heritage series that peaks in month 3 — and hands the rebrand launch a ready-made audience.

6.1

Four Content Pillars

Every piece in the 13-week calendar belongs to exactly one pillar. Specs come straight from the crawl — no invented numbers.

A

Fleet Authority

One “Know Your Stage” deep-dive per APEX model with real specs, a stage-size chooser (“which stage for 500 vs 5,000 people”), and all 9 working spec PDFs brought on-domain from apex.miami. The fleet is the product catalog — treat every stage like a product launch.

mobile stage rentals Miamistage rental South Floridahydraulic mobile stage rentalsAPEX mobile stages
B

Backstage / Behind the Build

Load-in timelapses, hydraulic lift shots, rigging close-ups, crew spotlights. “Backstage” already exists as your blog category — it becomes the Reels/YouTube engine. Zero writing burden: shoot on every load-in, publish weekly.

outdoor concert staging companyconcert production company
C

Organizer Playbooks

Hurricane-season contingency planning for outdoor events — a South Florida angle nobody else owns, in season right now — plus municipal 4th of July / festival timelines, permits & stage-dimension guides, and NYE booking lead times.

event production Miamicity event production Miamiturnkey event production services
D

50 Years of Reflections

Weekly archive/throwback series (est. ~1975) building to the rebrand-launch moment in month 3. Real history: Hialeah 4th of July, Big Orange NYE, the through-the-years archive. Heritage + horsepower, made visible.

Reflections Productions50 years on stage
6.2

Three Monthly Arcs

M1
Jul 15 – Aug 18 · Weeks 1–5

Fleet Authority + Hurricane Readiness

Week-1 hygiene fixes, hurricane contingency content while the season peaks, flagship deep-dives (5040, 4240), the stage-size chooser, the permits guide, spec sheets on-domain.

M2
Aug 19 – Sep 15 · Weeks 6–9

Booking-Season Push

NYE/holiday booking opens on Big Orange NYE lead time, fall festival prep timelines, remaining fleet deep-dives, and the APEX 4232 teaser campaign begins.

M3
Sep 16 – Oct 15 · Weeks 10–13

50th Anniversary Peak + APEX 4232 Launch

The anniversary series lands its payoff: 4232 launches with spec sheet and film, flagship case studies (Hialeah 4th, Big Orange NYE), rebrand reveal in week 13.

6.4

Channel Cadence

Blog / News
1 / week

The SEO engine. Every post targets one keyword, links to Quick Quote, and spawns the week’s social + GBP derivatives.

Instagram
3 / week

1 Reel (Backstage build footage) + 1 carousel (specs / playbook) + 1 archive post (50 Years series).

Facebook
Mirror + events

Mirrors IG; creates event listings for public shows. The municipal and community audience lives here.

YouTube
2 / month

Load-in timelapses, stage walkthroughs, crew spotlights, the 4232 launch film. Long-shelf-life search assets.

X (Twitter)
Light repost

1–2 reposts a week of Reels and blog links. No original content burden.

New Backstage Pass email
Monthly

Proposed new channel — your first owned email list. Issues Aug 18, Sep 15, Oct 13: booking prompts, one playbook, one archive story, one fleet feature.

Google Business Profile
1 / week

Weekly photo/offer post mirroring the blog. Prerequisite: unify NAP — the site currently publishes 2 addresses, 2 emails and 2 phones in 5+ formats.

6.5

What We Measure

+30%
Quote requests + calls by Oct 15 — primary KPI (baseline set weeks 1–2)
#1
Fleet pages as top organic landing group after the homepage
5
Tracked terms: top 10 for “mobile stage rentals Miami” + “stage rental South Florida”; top 20 for 3 more
+25%
Instagram followers, with Reels at 2× current reach by week 13
250
Backstage Pass subscribers — from zero, via site embeds + QR at events
6.6

Week-1 Quick Wins — Status

Originally scoped as pre-content hygiene. Four of six are already solved in the new build — they take effect the day the site goes live. Two remain as true launch-week tasks.

Retire the /seo/ page SOLVED AT LAUNCH

Doesn’t exist in the new build — goes dark with a redirect the day the domain flips.

Rewrite the About page DONE

Written from scratch in the new build: the 1975→2026 story, family of brands, hiring, values.

Fix the PRODUCITON typo sitewide SOLVED AT LAUNCH

The new build spells the company’s name correctly on every page; /potfolio/ gets its 301 at launch.

Unify NAP, then claim GBP LAUNCH WEEK

The new site uses one address, email and phone everywhere — citations and Google Business Profile still need the same cleanup.

Retire the ~20 theme-demo pages SOLVED AT LAUNCH

None of them exist in the new architecture — redirect map ships with the launch.

Bring spec sheets on-domain IN PROGRESS

The 5040’s 70-page package is already rebuilt as a web page; the remaining 7 stages and PDF rehosting are next-wave work.

07 · Process & Road Ahead

Four Builds.
One Week.

Every revision below is real and reviewable — a working site, not a mock-up. This is the pace and depth the full engagement runs at.

R1

Foundation

Complete crawl of the current site — 85 pages, 42 images, 84 documented issues. New identity system: palm + reflection-line brand, six logo concepts across two rounds. First working homepage with the mirrored “Fifty Years On Stage” hero.

R2

The Full Site

All 14 pages built and QA’d: the complete APEX fleet with real manufacturer specs, six production services with actual gear lists, a properly written 50-year story, and the drone hero video — recompressed from 30 MB to 3.9 MB so it starts instantly instead of stalling.

R3

Built to Convert

Homepage restructured around the visitor: “What do you need?” pathways, named-client trust strip, how-it-works, published rates surfaced from the archive onto their own page, and four clickable case studies — Hialeah alone carries 12 event photos and two aftermovies.

R4

Polish & Reach

Mobile hardened end-to-end (every page verified at phone width), social everywhere it matters with a live Instagram reel on the homepage, testimonial section, date-availability checker, engineering trust chips on every stage — and the APEX 5040’s 70-page PDF submittal rebuilt as a readable web spec, wind plan included. No one else in this industry has that.

If We Keep
Going

01

Every Stage, Torn Down

The 5040 web-spec treatment for all seven remaining stages — drawings, load maps and wind plans, readable on a phone at the permit counter.

02

Market Landing Pages

Dedicated pages for municipalities, festivals and broadcast — matched to how each buyer searches and what each needs to see.

03

Resources Hub

One technical library: all specs, permit & footprint guides, power requirements. The bookmark every event planner in South Florida keeps.

04

Booking Workflow

The date-checker wired to a live inbox with same-day response — plus quote-form delivery, analytics, and search-console setup at launch.

05

Content Engine

The 90-day editorial plan in tab 06, executed: weekly fleet features, organizer playbooks, the 50th-anniversary series, and the APEX 4232 launch.

06

Brand in the Wild

Crew uniforms, hats and flags in the new identity; team headshots; event-day photo & drone coverage; the new palm on every stage wing and trailer. The rebrand, visible at every show.

07

3D Stage Viewer

Interactive 3D models of the fleet — the one feature the industry leader has that this site doesn’t. Yet.
